channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-05
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. In the scienceverse/metacheck project, significant improvements were made to various branches, including the addition of a bibr-JSON REST API and enhancements to the plumber and data checking tools. Notably, these updates have improved the handling of bibliographic data and large language models, demonstrating the project's commitment to robust and transparent research tools. Meanwhile, the JamieCummins/regcheck project simplified its staging branch by removing the sign-up feature, streamlining the user experience. These developments highlight the connections between our projects, as improved data checking and management in metacheck can inform and support other tools like regcheck. Overall, our community is making great strides in advancing open-source research tools, and we look forward to seeing the continued impact of these efforts.

75 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: statcheck

Most active:

docpluck31 commits
regcheck18 commits
metacheck16 commits
escicheck6 commits
statcheck2 commits

item.title: metacheck: 12 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

Work was done on the `data_check`, `jw_llm_fixes`, `jw_plumber_bibr_api`, `jw_bibr_read_passthrough`, `jw_grobid_text_fix`, `jw_schema_sync_10_5`, and `jw_fable_fixes` branches. On the `data_check` branch, "improvements" were made by Lakens, and later, "Transparent resource caps, LLM/download controls, data-manifest + archive tooling" were added. The `jw_plumber_bibr_api` branch saw the addition of a "bibr-JSON REST API — validated uploads, HTML report, hardened runtime" by Jakub Werner. The `jw_fable_fixes` branch had multiple commits, including fixes to the "code_check green path, ref_miscitation report, traffic lights, stale imports" and the addition of a "DRY endpoint pipeline, /modules endpoint, report=false, hardened Docker runtime" to the plumber. The `jw_llm_fixes` branch corrected the "ollama-native gate (&& not %%)", and the `jw_bibr_read_passthrough` and `jw_grobid_text_fix` branches allowed "bibr abstract and figure/table captions" to flow through and stopped "anchor normalization from corrupting paper text", respectively.

12 commit(s) today:

  • ad4cb14 [data_check] improvements — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: statcheck: activity update
item.description:
MicheleNuijten/statcheck

The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was worked on, where a merge was performed from the same branch of the remote repository. The merge commit was made to integrate changes from the remote `feature-non-apa-newer` branch into the local `feature-non-apa-newer` branch. Additionally, on the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, "admin updates" were made, as per the commit message. No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The changes were made by MicheleNuijten. These commits did not introduce any new features or fixes beyond the mentioned admin updates and the merge operation.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
